cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer here. The question is asking what we can learn from the passage. The story is about a farmer in central China who uses an old horse to plow his fields. When the horse dies, everyone feels bad, but the farmer just says "We'll see." Then, when the same thing happens with a new horse, and even when his son breaks his leg, he keeps saying the same thing. The twist comes when the army doesn't recruit his son because of the broken leg, which turns out to be a good thing. So, the main theme here seems to be about not jumping to conclusions about good or bad events because things might turn out differently later. The farmer remains calm and doesn't overreact, which the story's moral at the end reinforces. The question is asking which of the options (A to D) is correct based on the passage. Looking
